---
format: memo-typst
title: "MEMORANDUM"
memo-to: "Prof. Department Faculty"
memo-from: "Dr. Jane Doe, Chair"
subject: "Spring 2026 Scheduling and Course Load Reminders"
date: "2026-04-21"
sender-name: "Dr. Jane Doe"
sender-qualifications: "PhD"
sender-position: "Associate Professor and Chair"
sender-department: "Department of Example Studies"
sender-institution: "Your University | City, ST"
sender-email: "jane.doe@university.edu"
sender-phone: "(555) 555-0100"
sender-website: "janedoe.example"
# dept-header: "College of Liberal Arts and Communication"
# dept-subheader: "Example Studies | PO Box 1234, City ST 00000 | 555-555-0100"
# logo-header: "assets/department-logo.png"
# logo-footer: "assets/accreditation-mark.png"
# author-sig: true
# signature: "assets/signature.png"
# cosig: true
# cosig-name: "Dr. Dean Name"
# cosig-title: "Dean, College of Liberal Arts"
---

This memo summarizes a few items I want to be sure everyone has on their
radar as we wrap up the spring semester and head into summer planning.

## Scheduling

Please submit your availability for fall teaching by May 15. I know a few
of you are working through research leaves and course-release requests;
bring those to me directly so I can factor them into the schedule.

## Course Load

As a reminder, the standard load for AY 2026–27 is 3/3 for tenure-track
faculty and 4/4 for lecturers, with reductions per your contract. If you
have questions about how any of this applies to your individual situation,
please let me know.

## Summer Travel

Anyone traveling on department or grant funds this summer should submit
travel authorizations at least two weeks prior to travel. The university's
reimbursement processing has slowed this year, so the earlier the better.

If any of the above needs further discussion, please reach out. I am happy
to meet in person or by email to work through any questions.

```{=typst}
#ps[PS: The next department meeting is scheduled for May 8 at 2:00 PM.]
```
